verdict_reasoning: HSPD1 (HSP60) is canonically a mitochondrial matrix chaperonin with no TM domain, GPI anchor, or conventional signal peptide. ~80% of the protein resides in the mitochondrial matrix. However, a well-documented minority pool translocates to the outer plasma membrane under stress, inflammatory states, and oncogenic transformation — confirmed by immunogold EM on tumor cell lines showing HSP60 at the plasma-cell membrane. This cancer/stress-induced ecto-display qualifies as cell_state_induced surface accessibility. The surface pool is absent or negligible at baseline in non-transformed cells.
